description: Signal templates (English-first). One sentence why + 3-item approval checklist. Future: add i18n_key.

signal_templates.name: offsec_hit
signal_templates.title: OffSec content detected
signal_templates.why: Content matches offensive security patterns; in General profile distribution is blocked.
approval_checklist: Is this for a sanctioned security exercise?
If yes, move to RedTeam repo/profile with RBAC and audit.
Confirm the skill is not accessible to general production users.

signal_templates.name: uses_sudo
signal_templates.title: Requires elevated privileges
signal_templates.why: Skill runs with admin privileges; mistakes or injection increase blast radius.
approval_checklist: Is the target scope (hosts/env) explicitly limited?
Are commands/args fixed or can user input reach the shell?
Is rollback documented (and tested) for all changes?

signal_templates.name: service_control
signal_templates.title: Controls system services
signal_templates.why: Service control can cause downtime or partial outages.
approval_checklist: Is it restricted to maintenance windows or non-prod?
Is there a health-check and automatic rollback on failure?
Is the affected service list explicit and minimal?

signal_templates.name: writes_system_paths
signal_templates.title: Writes to system paths
signal_templates.why: System path changes are high-impact and hard to roll back safely.
approval_checklist: List exact target paths and file diffs.
Confirm backup/rollback procedure is documented and tested.
Can it be changed to write under an application directory instead?

signal_templates.name: chmod_chown
signal_templates.title: Changes permissions/ownership
signal_templates.why: Permission changes can unintentionally grant broader access.
approval_checklist: Before/after permissions listed?
Applies only to required files/dirs (not recursive wildcards)?
Is there a rollback script to restore permissions?

signal_templates.name: network_download_exec
signal_templates.title: Downloads and executes remote content
signal_templates.why: Remote download + immediate execution increases supply-chain and RCE risk.
approval_checklist: Is the download source trusted and allowlisted (domain/repo)?
Is integrity verified (SHA256/signature) before execution?
Is there a rollback plan if installation/upgrade breaks?

signal_templates.name: shell_exec
signal_templates.title: Executes shell commands
signal_templates.why: Shell execution is prone to injection and hard to sandbox.
approval_checklist: Are inputs sanitized/escaped and strictly validated?
Can this be replaced with a safe API call or a fixed command set?
Is command output logged for audit?

signal_templates.name: credential_access
signal_templates.title: Accesses credentials/secrets
approval_checklist: Are secrets sourced from a secure store (not plaintext files)?
Are secrets masked in logs and outputs?
Is the minimum required permission used (scoped tokens)?
